category_title: Non-ambulant adolescent with Rett Syndrome and profound learning difficulties communicating via eye gaze and vocalizations
minimal_context: Evie is participating in an hour-long art session with an adult at school.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our hour-long art session. Here are the materials for you to use.

STUDENT: (Looks at the art materials and makes a vocalization, reaching slightly but stopping)

TUTOR: What do you want to do with them?

STUDENT: (Looks away from the materials, becoming disengaged and making a frustrated vocalization)

TUTOR: We need to keep working on the art activity.

STUDENT: (Pushes the materials away, begins to cry, and stops making any further initiations to communicate)

options.A: Stay calm and confident when dealing with her control issues during the activity.
options.B: Offer both developmentally appropriate art activities and offer her pieces of a banana.
options.C: Give her a warning about the materials but do not discipline her further.
options.D: Echo her vocalization to lead to a series of chanted conversational exchanges.
